pattern: Checking a bijection between pattern letters and whitespace-separated words means enforcing the mapping in both directions with two dictionaries so neither a letter nor a word is ever reused for a different counterpart.
hints: Split the sentence into words and require the word count to equal the pattern length before anything else.
A one-way map from letters to words is not enough; two different letters must not map to the same word, so guard both directions.
Walk the letters and words in lockstep keeping a letter to word map and a word to letter map, and on any conflict between an existing binding and the current pair return false.

edge_inputs.description: valid bijection abba over four words
pattern ends in a different word, breaks the letter-to-word map
two letters cannot both map to dog, violates injectivity
length mismatch, more words than letters
length mismatch, more letters than words
same word forced onto distinct letters
longer valid mapping with three symbols
input: red blue green red blue green

stress.description: random patterns paired with words built from a shared vocabulary so both valid and invalid mappings occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    letters = 'abcde'
    vocab = ['dog', 'cat', 'fish', 'bird', 'cow', 'red', 'blue']
    pat = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    words = [rng.choice(vocab) for _ in range(n)]
    return [pat, ' '.join(words)]
